subject: College Biology
question: Which of the following is a frameshift mutation?

options.A: CAT HAS HIS → CAT HAS HIT
options.B: CAT HAS HIS → CAT HSH ISA
options.C: CAT HAS HIS → CAT HIS HAT
options.D: CAT HAS HIS → CAT WAS HIT

analysis: Step 1: Choice A (CAT HAS HIS → CAT HAS HIT) depicts a point substitution mutation where a single letter is swapped without disrupting the three-letter grouping; since the reading frame remains intact, this is not a frameshift mutation.

Step 2: Choice B (CAT HAS HIS → CAT HSH ISA) illustrates an insertion or deletion event that breaks the original triplet boundaries, forcing all downstream letters to regroup into new triplets (HSH and ISA); this misalignment of the reading frame is the defining characteristic of a frameshift mutation.

Step 3: Choice C (CAT HAS HIS → CAT HIS HAT) represents a chromosomal rearrangement or inversion of entire codons/words rather than a nucleotide addition or loss, meaning the reading frame itself is not shifted.

Step 4: Choice D (CAT HAS HIS → CAT WAS HIT) involves multiple independent point substitutions that change individual letters but preserve the original three-letter segmentation, so the reading frame remains unchanged and it does not qualify as a frameshift mutation.

Step 5: After evaluating all candidates sequentially, only Choice B demonstrates the critical mechanism of a frameshift mutation, where an indel event alters the reading frame and consequently scrambles all subsequent codons.
